clusterPriorities.rationale: Highest overlap with public coding skill ecosystems and most likely to benefit from broader benchmark tightening.
Current shapes are good, but public breadth can still improve engine, platform, and architecture decision boundaries.
Language packs are stable, but the public corpus can still identify which need deeper sidecars or should remain intentionally lean.
Broader public workflow and installer ecosystems are likely to surface the most net-new candidate skills here.
Everything-scope research should still surface non-coding support families even if implementation happens later.

currentSkills.name: auth-architect
currentSkills.description: Use when designing or reviewing authentication and authorization for backend systems, including sessions, tokens, OAuth or OIDC, RBAC or ABAC, passkeys, service-to-service auth, and policy enforcement boundaries.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the auth and policy specialist and preserve a clear split from generic security review, generic backend implementation, and API-contract-only work.
currentSkills.keep: Sessions, tokens, OAuth or OIDC, passkeys, RBAC or ABAC, tenant isolation, and service-to-service auth as the core trigger surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public auth-heavy skill families for gaps around account recovery, webhook verification, and managed identity-provider platform tradeoffs without broadening into generic security review.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus session or token policy checklist; add templates only if auth-flow review outputs become standardized.
publicSignals: Auth consistently reappears across backend and security skill libraries as a distinct decision surface rather than a footnote inside framework or security-general skills.

currentSkills.name: fastapi-expert
currentSkills.description: Use when building FastAPI services with async Python, Pydantic v2, lifespan-managed resources, dependency-based auth, background task boundaries, and OpenAPI-safe request handling.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the async Python service specialist and preserve the Pydantic-v2 plus boundary-first framing.
currentSkills.keep: FastAPI-specific async correctness, Pydantic boundaries, dependencies, and OpenAPI hygiene as the core trigger surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public FastAPI libraries for missing background-job, lifespan, or auth-depth references without broadening into generic Python service guidance.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus async/Pydantic checklist; add templates only if service skeleton outputs become standard.
publicSignals: FastAPI skills are most valuable when they stay clearly separate from generic Python and generic API design.

currentSkills.name: graphql-architect
currentSkills.description: Use when designing GraphQL schemas, resolver boundaries, batching strategy, auth and policy enforcement, federation shape, query safety, and real-time graph behavior.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the graph-specific API specialist and maintain a clear separation from REST contract and generic backend framework guidance.
currentSkills.keep: Schema shape, nullability, resolver boundaries, batching, auth, and federation tradeoffs as the core trigger surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public graph-focused skill libraries for whether subscriptions or GraphQL security deserve deeper sidecars or adjacent candidates.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus schema/resolver checklist; no scripts yet.
publicSignals: Public graph skills are strongest when they explicitly separate schema architecture from generic API design.

currentSkills.name: nestjs-expert
currentSkills.description: Use when building NestJS services that need strong module boundaries, dependency injection, DTO validation, guards or interceptors, auth boundaries, transport-aware module design, and production-safe TypeScript backend structure.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the Nest-specific backend specialist and preserve a clear split from generic Node and generic API skills.
currentSkills.keep: Modules, DI, DTOs, guards, interceptors, and transport-vs-domain boundaries as the core surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Nest and TypeScript backend libraries for missing monorepo, messaging, or auth-depth references.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus module/DTO/guard checklist; no scripts yet.
publicSignals: Nest-specific patterns are common enough in public ecosystems to justify a dedicated specialist.

currentSkills.name: nodejs-best-practices
currentSkills.description: Use for modern Node.js backend architecture, runtime choices, worker or queue boundaries, edge-vs-server tradeoffs, reliability controls, and production-safe service implementation in the current LTS era.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the Node runtime specialist and preserve explicit runtime and reliability boundaries.
currentSkills.keep: Runtime choice, reliability controls, event-loop safety, graceful shutdown, and operational posture.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public runtime-oriented skill libraries for missing worker, queue, or edge/serverless sidecar guidance.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus runtime/reliability checklist; scripts later only if service validation becomes standardized.
publicSignals: Stronger public Node skills focus on runtime and reliability, not generic backend advice alone.

currentSkills.name: architecture-designer
currentSkills.description: Use when designing or reviewing system architecture, interface boundaries, tradeoffs, ADRs, scalability posture, and the smallest durable target shape for a product or platform.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the primary high-level architecture skill and preserve the product-first, anti-overengineering stance.
currentSkills.keep: Boundary, tradeoff, and ADR-style decision framing as the core skill boundary.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public system-design and planning libraries for gaps around rollout posture, observability-by-design, and infrastructure coupling tradeoffs.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus system tradeoff checklist; add templates only if ADR output becomes standardized.
publicSignals: Public system-design skills are often too vague or too diagram-heavy; stronger examples stay decision-focused.

currentSkills.name: database-design
currentSkills.description: Use when designing schemas, relationships, indexes, ORM boundaries, and safe migration plans for relational or document-oriented workloads.
currentSkills.decision: Keep schema and migration planning separate from performance tuning and engine selection.
currentSkills.keep: Entities, keys, indexes, pagination, backfills, and rollback as the core trigger surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public libraries for whether local-first, event-sourced, or document-heavy modeling patterns justify more sidecar depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus schema-migration checklist; add templates only if migration planning output becomes standardized.
publicSignals: Public repositories that mix design and tuning guidance tend to become broad and noisy.

currentSkills.name: database-optimizer
currentSkills.description: Use for database performance triage across engines: query-plan analysis, indexing changes, wait and contention diagnosis, config tuning, and safe before/after validation.
currentSkills.decision: Keep this as the evidence-first tuning specialist and maintain the strict before/after measurement stance.
currentSkills.keep: Plans, waits, contention, index tradeoffs, and rollback-aware tuning as the core boundary.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public performance skill libraries for whether lock-triage, query-shape, and config-tuning references should be split further.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus query-triage checklist; no scripts until a standard measurement workflow is worth encoding.
publicSignals: Public performance skills are strongest when they stay evidence-first and rollback-aware.

currentSkills.name: database-skills
currentSkills.description: Use as the primary database hub for engine choice, schema-vs-tuning routing, migration safety framing, and database task triage before engine-specific deep dives.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the routing hub and preserve the current hub-plus-specialist structure instead of collapsing engines or tuning back into one mega-skill.
currentSkills.keep: Engine routing, schema-vs-tuning triage, and migration-risk framing at the hub level.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public database skill libraries for missing managed-database, vector, or data-access companion families before broadening the hub itself.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus routing reference; no scripts until engine discovery or validation can be standardized.
publicSignals: The better public pattern is a hub plus narrow engine or concern specialists rather than one giant database manual.

currentSkills.name: microservices-architect
currentSkills.description: Use when decomposing systems into services, designing service boundaries, choosing sync and async integration patterns, and managing distributed-system reliability tradeoffs.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the distributed-boundary specialist and preserve the anti-premature-microservices stance.
currentSkills.keep: Service seams, sync-vs-async boundaries, reliability, and distributed tradeoffs as the core surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public workflow and architecture libraries for whether event-driven design or API gateway concerns justify adjacent specialists rather than broadening this skill.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus service-boundary checklist; no scripts or templates yet.
publicSignals: Public microservice skills often romanticize distribution; stronger patterns stay ownership- and failure-mode-driven.

currentSkills.name: mongodb
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically MongoDB: document-model design, aggregation pipelines, index strategy, shard-key tradeoffs, and operational guidance for document workloads.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the document-database specialist and maintain a clear embed-vs-reference and aggregation-focused boundary.
currentSkills.keep: Document shape, aggregation, validation, indexing, and shard-key tradeoffs as the core trigger surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public document-db skill families for whether vector, Atlas-specific, or event-source document guidance should stay separate.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus MongoDB checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Document-database skills justify their own surface when they stay centered on document-boundary and shard-key tradeoffs.

currentSkills.name: mysql
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically MySQL or InnoDB: schema/index design, transaction behavior, replication-aware migrations, query tuning, and managed MySQL operational tradeoffs.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the MySQL specialist and preserve the InnoDB plus operational rollout boundary.
currentSkills.keep: InnoDB behavior, indexes, Online DDL, replication-aware rollouts, and transaction tradeoffs.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public MySQL and managed-MySQL families for gaps around hosted variants or sharded adjacencies.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus MySQL checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Public data libraries repeatedly justify a separate MySQL specialist where operational topology changes the answer.

currentSkills.name: neki
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically Neki, PlanetScale’s sharded Postgres architecture: shard-key design, distributed Postgres tradeoffs, routing constraints, and operational decisions for large-scale Postgres workloads.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as experimental, narrow, and assumption-sensitive until the public corpus around Neki-like sharded Postgres patterns becomes stronger.
currentSkills.keep: Sharded Postgres assumptions, routing, and uncertainty handling as the core boundary.
currentSkills.change: Refresh this skill against public material more frequently than the others and avoid broadening it without stronger evidence.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus Neki checklist only.
publicSignals: Public corpus support for Neki-specific advice is still limited compared with more mature database families.

currentSkills.name: postgres
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically PostgreSQL: schema design with Postgres features, query plans, indexes, JSONB, extensions, partitioning, logical replication, or managed Postgres operational tradeoffs.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the Postgres specialist and preserve the plain-relational-first posture.
currentSkills.keep: Indexes, JSONB, extensions, locking, migration safety, and operational tradeoffs.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Postgres-focused families for gaps around pgvector, branching, or managed-Postgres platform adjacencies.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus Postgres checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Postgres-specific operational and feature tradeoffs change answers enough to justify a dedicated specialist.

currentSkills.name: redis
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically Redis: cache design, key strategy, TTLs, queues, rate limiting, pub/sub, memory tradeoffs, and safe persistence or eviction decisions.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the cache and in-memory coordination specialist instead of folding it into general database guidance.
currentSkills.keep: TTL, invalidation, queues, rate limits, eviction, persistence, and hot-key risk.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Redis families for missing stream, distributed-lock, or cache-coherence sidecars.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus Redis checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Public libraries consistently treat Redis as a distinct concern because cache and coordination semantics differ from primary data stores.

currentSkills.name: sqlite
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically SQLite or libSQL/Turso-style SQLite: local-first schema design, WAL/concurrency tradeoffs, embedded deployments, and lightweight migration strategy.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the local-first and embedded database specialist.
currentSkills.keep: WAL, concurrency, local-first deployment, embedded constraints, and migration posture.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public SQLite and Turso/libSQL-oriented families for whether sync or offline-first sidecars deserve more depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus SQLite checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Local-first and embedded constraints are distinct enough that SQLite benefits from a dedicated specialist.

currentSkills.name: supabase
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically Supabase: managed Postgres with RLS, auth, storage, edge functions, branching, and product decisions that depend on Supabase platform behavior.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the platform-aware Postgres specialist and preserve a clear split from plain Postgres guidance.
currentSkills.keep: RLS, auth, storage, platform coupling, and managed rollout constraints.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Supabase and platform-coupled skills for missing branching, realtime, or edge-function depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus Supabase checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Managed-platform database skills repeatedly justify their own surface where platform policy and auth change the answer.

currentSkills.name: vitess
currentSkills.description: Use when the task is specifically Vitess or Vitess-backed MySQL platforms: shard-key design, vindexes, resharding, query-routing constraints, Online DDL, and operational tradeoffs for horizontally scaled MySQL.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the sharded-MySQL specialist and preserve the explicit shard-key and routing focus.
currentSkills.keep: Shard keys, vindexes, Online DDL, resharding, and routing constraints.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public sharded-data and platform libraries for whether query-routing or resharding references need more depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus Vitess checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Even though examples are rarer, the operational surface is distinct enough that Vitess should stay separate.

currentSkills.name: c-pro
currentSkills.description: Use for modern C23-era systems and embedded engineering with memory safety discipline, build reproducibility, and low-level debugging awareness.
currentSkills.decision: Keep this as a language-level canonical and resist framework bleed. Use the broader public corpus to decide whether targeted references or adjacent companion skills are warranted.
currentSkills.keep: Retain the current language-first boundary and keep framework/runtime specifics outside the root language skill.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public prevalence, add or refine references only when repeated tactical depth is clearly justified, and avoid turning language skills into giant ecosystem catalogs.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep lean for now; add references only if embedded or systems-specific tactical depth is repeatedly demanded.
publicSignals: Language skills remain common across public ecosystems, but the stronger public pattern is to keep them lean and let framework or domain specialists handle depth.

currentSkills.decision: Keep this as a language-level canonical and resist framework bleed. Use the broader public corpus to decide whether targeted references or adjacent companion skills are warranted.

currentSkills.name: typescript-pro

currentSkills.decision: Keep this as a language-level canonical and resist framework bleed. Use the broader public corpus to decide whether targeted references or adjacent companion skills are warranted.

currentSkills.change: Recheck public prevalence, add or refine references only when repeated tactical depth is clearly justified, and avoid turning language skills into giant ecosystem catalogs.

currentSkills.name: skill-creator
currentSkills.description: Use when creating, rebuilding, or repairing a Foundry skill package, workflow skill, or agent-facing skill surface across Codex, Claude, Copilot, Antigravity, Cursor, and Windsurf. Do not use for prompt-only tweaks or edits to one generated mirror.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the canonical authoring skill, but deepen its research-refresh and cross-platform packaging playbook as the broader public corpus grows.
currentSkills.keep: Canonical-source-first packaging, sidecar discipline, mirror regeneration, and no third-party copying.
currentSkills.change: Add a repeatable public-research refresh workflow, corpus review guidance, and clearer rules for when a new skill warrants scripts, templates, or adjacent companion skills.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus references and scripts; consider adding a reusable research-template or scaffold template once the new corpus is stable.
publicSignals: Public ecosystems repeatedly need a skill-authoring or installer surface, but quality varies widely and reinforces the need for a stricter Foundry-owned creator skill.

currentSkills.name: api-designer
currentSkills.description: Use when defining or reviewing external API contracts, OpenAPI specifications, resource models, pagination, versioning, and error response standards. Do not use for pure database design or framework-only handler wiring.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the canonical contract-first skill and continue separating it from framework and database implementation concerns.
currentSkills.keep: Resource modeling, versioning, pagination, idempotency, and error semantics as the primary trigger boundary.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public API-oriented skill families for missing edge cases such as event APIs, long-running operations, and public-vs-internal contract split guidance.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep the current root plus checklist reference; add more references only if contract examples or async API guidance become necessary.
publicSignals: Strong public pattern for keeping contract design separate from framework implementation.

currentSkills.name: api-patterns
currentSkills.description: Use when choosing between REST, GraphQL, and type-safe RPC patterns, or when standardizing response envelopes, versioning, pagination, auth shape, and API protection strategy.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the pattern-choice companion skill and continue separating REST-vs-GraphQL-vs-RPC tradeoffs from endpoint implementation.
currentSkills.keep: Pattern selection, response vocabulary, auth shape, and versioning strategy as the core surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public API libraries for whether webhooks, streaming, or event-driven interfaces deserve a distinct adjacent candidate skill rather than broadening this one.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus pattern checklist; no scripts or templates yet.
publicSignals: Public repos repeatedly split protocol or transport choice from framework-specific code.

currentSkills.name: design-system-builder
currentSkills.description: Use when creating or reviewing reusable UI components, token-driven styling, variant APIs, theming hooks, and component documentation for a design system.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the reusable-component and token API skill; do not let it absorb one-off product UI guidance.
currentSkills.keep: System-vs-product boundary, token-driven APIs, variants, theming, and docs as the core boundary.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public component-system skills for missing slot/composition or accessibility contract guidance.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus component API checklist; templates may be added later if component spec outputs become standardized.
publicSignals: Public UI-system skills degrade when they collapse reusable-system concerns and app-specific UI decisions together.

currentSkills.name: frontend-design
currentSkills.description: Use for web UI design decisions: layout, hierarchy, typography, color, motion, and interaction clarity. This skill teaches decision-making, not a fixed visual style.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as a UI decision-making skill, not a style bundle or aesthetic prompt pack.
currentSkills.keep: Hierarchy, typography, color, motion, and interaction-state decision logic.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public design skills for whether accessibility critique, state-flow review, and design review deserve adjacent specialists such as frontend-code-review.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus hierarchy/state checklist; no scripts or templates yet.
publicSignals: Public design skills often drift into vague aesthetic advice; the stronger pattern is decision and state clarity.

currentSkills.name: nextjs-developer
currentSkills.description: Use for implementing and reviewing Next.js App Router features, React Server Components and Actions, caching strategy, SEO, and production runtime behavior.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the Next.js framework specialist and preserve route-level rendering plus cache decisions as the core boundary.
currentSkills.keep: App Router, RSC, Server Actions, caching, SEO, and route-level runtime behavior.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Next-specific libraries for missing partial prerendering, streaming, or deployment-path references without absorbing general React advice.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus App Router/cache playbook; add templates only if route-spec outputs become repeatable.
publicSignals: Vercel and community ecosystems strongly justify a dedicated Next framework skill rather than folding Next into generic React.

currentSkills.name: react-expert
currentSkills.description: Use for framework-agnostic React architecture with React 19 patterns, state design, component boundaries, and rendering-performance decisions.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the React runtime specialist and preserve the framework-agnostic boundary.
currentSkills.keep: Component boundaries, state placement, effects, rendering cost, and interaction correctness.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public React families for missing compiler, form-action, or Server Component adjacency guidance without turning this into a Next skill.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus rendering/state checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Public React skills are strongest when they stay runtime-focused instead of merging design, framework, and tooling into one pack.

currentSkills.name: tailwind-patterns
currentSkills.description: Use for Tailwind CSS v4 architecture, CSS-first theme setup, utility composition, container-query usage, and design-token hygiene.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the styling-system specialist and preserve a clear split from visual design and framework architecture.
currentSkills.keep: Token setup, composition, container queries, and class ergonomics as the core surface.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public Tailwind skill families for whether design-token sync or component-extraction references need more depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus token/composition checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: Public CSS/Tailwind skills are most useful when they separate system hygiene from visual-direction work.

currentSkills.name: web-perf
currentSkills.description: Use for measuring and improving web performance with Core Web Vitals, rendering-path analysis, bundle and network prioritization, and framework-aware delivery tradeoffs.
currentSkills.decision: Keep as the browser-performance specialist and preserve a strict measurement-first boundary.
currentSkills.keep: CWV, bundle/network/render triage, and framework-aware performance tradeoffs.
currentSkills.change: Recheck public performance libraries for whether lab-vs-field, profiling, or server/bundle split references need more depth.
currentSkills.packagingNext: Keep root plus CWV triage checklist; no scripts.
publicSignals: The strongest public pattern is still measure-first, then targeted remediation with explicit tradeoffs.

newCandidateSkills.rationale: Repeated public demand around browser automation and validation-heavy coding work justifies a dedicated E2E skill.
Public skill ecosystems repeatedly separate UI and UX review from implementation and generic code review.
Typed TypeScript database stacks recur often enough that an ORM-specific specialist may be warranted.
GitHub automation, PR flow, branch hygiene, and CI interactions show up frequently in public dev-skill libraries.
PR drafting and review-prep appear often enough to justify a narrow workflow skill if GitHub automation becomes first-class.
Debugging is broad enough to deserve a methodology-focused skill separate from language or framework implementation skills.
Public repos frequently separate browser validation from generic QA or unit-test guidance.
MCP and agent-tooling construction appears repeatedly enough to justify a first-class Foundry skill family.
Documentation upkeep is a recurring support family in public skill libraries and maps well to workflow-driven maintenance.
Research and evidence synthesis appear across many public skill packs and can complement coding and architecture work.
